evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~13 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 2/10 — Reads entirely as corporate editorial output with no individual voice.

suggestedPatch.target: This closes the loop between clinical insight and pragmatic, compassionate everyday wellness checks.
suggestedPatch.before: This closes the loop between clinical insight and pragmatic, compassionate everyday wellness checks.
suggestedPatch.after: I tell my clients: one blood panel plus one honest conversation beats a year of vague resolutions.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 2/10: Reads entirely as corporate editorial output with no individual voice.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 2/10 — Every claim is hedged, generic, and risk-free.

suggestedPatch.target: A pragmatic Q1 health check is your moment to non-judgementally assess, adapt, and refocus for the rest of the year, ensuring goals are both clinically relevant and sustainable with the Dubai routine.
suggestedPatch.before: A pragmatic Q1 health check is your moment to non-judgementally assess, adapt, and refocus for the rest of the year, ensuring goals are both clinically relevant and sustainable with the Dubai routine.
suggestedPatch.after: Annual screenings are mostly theatre—useful once, useless for behaviour change. Quarterly reviews are where the real work happens.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 2/10: Every claim is hedged, generic, and risk-free.

evidence: Unexpected phrasing 2/10 — Relies on tired wellness clichés like 'progress not perfection' and 'close the loop'.

suggestedPatch.target: Progress, not perfection, is the clinically proven path.
suggestedPatch.before: Progress, not perfection, is the clinically proven path.
suggestedPatch.after: Miss a target? Treat it like a failed A/B test, not a character flaw.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasing" scored 2/10: Relies on tired wellness clichés like 'progress not perfection' and 'close the loop'.

evidence: Specific citations 5/10 — Mentions DHA, MOHAP, BMJ, Lancet and one price, but citations are unlinked placeholders and details are thin.

suggestedPatch.target: If your DHA screening is due, book it, some packages in Dubai start from AED 1,200, but options vary.
suggestedPatch.before: If your DHA screening is due, book it, some packages in Dubai start from AED 1,200, but options vary.
suggestedPatch.after: DHA-linked panels at Valeo start from AED 1,200; Aster's comparable package is AED 999 as of March 2026.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific citations" scored 5/10: Mentions DHA, MOHAP, BMJ, Lancet and one price, but citations are unlinked placeholders and details are thin.

evidence: Point of view 3/10 — Surveys guidelines evenly without taking a stance on what actually matters.

suggestedPatch.target: A pragmatic Q1 health check in Dubai reviews your 2026 wellness goals using clinically grounded self-audit prompts.
suggestedPatch.before: A pragmatic Q1 health check in Dubai reviews your 2026 wellness goals using clinically grounded self-audit prompts.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip the January reset ritual—if you haven't reviewed your goals by late March, you've already lost the year.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view" scored 3/10: Surveys guidelines evenly without taking a stance on what actually matters.

evidence: Quotable sentence 2/10 — Nothing memorable; language is bureaucratic and brand-laden.

suggestedPatch.target: Clinically grounded, non-judgemental check-ins, supported by Valeo Health’s at-home packages, help UAE residents make realistic health gains.
suggestedPatch.before: Clinically grounded, non-judgemental check-ins, supported by Valeo Health’s at-home packages, help UAE residents make realistic health gains.

evidence: Intro earns attention 3/10 — Opens with a generic framework promise rather than a hook.

suggestedPatch.target: With Q1 2026 behind us, Dubai residents can review progress on new year health goals in 15 minutes using this practical, evidence-backed audit framework.
suggestedPatch.before: With Q1 2026 behind us, Dubai residents can review progress on new year health goals in 15 minutes using this practical, evidence-backed audit framework.
suggestedPatch.after: Most of your New Year health goals are already dead. Here's the 15-minute Q1 autopsy before Dubai's summer makes recovery harder.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 3/10: Opens with a generic framework promise rather than a hook.

evidence: specificity 6/10 — Sporadic specificity exists (AED 1,200 screening cost, taraweeh mention, late-March heat timing) but the nutrition and movement checklist items routinely fall back on abstract descriptors like 'balanced,' 'region-specific needs,' and 'heart-healthy fats' without naming actual foods, gram targets, or measurable thresholds. The hydration section similarly says 'enough water' without a quantity. These are the zones where abstraction dominates and an editor should push for named foods, MOHAP-cited numbers, or concrete frequency targets.

suggestedPatch.target: Are meals balanced for region-specific needs (fibre, heart-healthy fats, less added sugar or salt)?
suggestedPatch.before: Are meals balanced for region-specific needs (fibre, heart-healthy fats, less added sugar or salt)?
suggestedPatch.after: Are your meals hitting MOHAP's UAE plate targets — roughly 50% vegetables and legumes, 25% whole grains such as brown rice or wholemeal khubz, and 25% lean protein — while staying under 5 g of added salt daily?
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: Sporadic specificity exists (AED 1,200 screening cost, taraweeh mention, late-March heat timing) but the nutrition and movement checklist items routinely fall back on abstract descriptors like 'balanced,' 'region-specific needs,' and 'heart-healthy fats' without naming actual foods, gram targets, or measurable thresholds. The hydration section similarly says 'enough water' without a quantity. These are the zones where abstraction dominates and an editor should push for named foods, MOHAP-cited numbers, or concrete frequency targets.
